title: AI-Strategic Node Framework (AI-SNF): Conceptual and Methodological White Book
subtitle: Version 0.1 Foundational Release
title_normalized: ai strategic node framework ai snf conceptual and methodological white book

creators.name: Wu, Shao-Yuan
creators.affiliation: Global AI Governance and Policy Research Center, EPINOVA LLC

alternate_identifiers.description: Internal EPINOVA Index White Book identifier
Version identifier for the AI-Strategic Node Framework foundational release
Version identifier for the bounded AI-Strategic Node Index diagnostic output specified within AI-SNF

description: This white book introduces the AI-Strategic Node Framework (AI-SNF) and its bounded composite diagnostic output, the AI-Strategic Node Index (AI-SNI) v0.1. It defines AI-strategic nodes as geographic, infrastructural, or institutional configurations whose position within AI-mediated perception, prediction, decision, governance, and resource-compute systems creates disproportionate strategic consequence. The white book specifies five dimensions, indicator logic, normalization and aggregation rules, exposure bands, diagnostic extensions, structural class attribution, typologies, uncertainty treatment, reporting templates, and interpretive guardrails.
abstract: The AI-Strategic Node Framework (AI-SNF) White Book develops a governance-oriented analytical architecture for diagnosing structural leverage, fragility, control asymmetry, and systemic consequence within AI-mediated systems. Rather than ranking countries or measuring aggregate AI capacity, AI-SNF shifts the unit of analysis to strategic nodes: territories, corridors, facility clusters, chokepoints, and infrastructural configurations whose disruption, degradation, capture, or misgovernance may produce disproportionate effects across sensing, prediction, decision-making, governance, and long-horizon resource-data-compute coupling. The framework organizes assessment around five dimensions: Algorithmic Sensing and Early-Warning Centrality (D1), Predictive Model Leverage and Dependency (D2), Decision-Loop Temporal Advantage (D3), Infrastructure-Governance Asymmetry and Control (D4), and Resource-Data-Compute Coupling Potential (D5). It specifies AI-SNI as a bounded composite diagnostic output for visualization and pattern recognition, not as a predictive model, ranking mechanism, or decision-automation tool. It further introduces exposure tier bands, diagnostic extensions for governance fragility and weakest-link sensitivity, a non-computable Structural Class System (S/A/B/C), international node typologies, evidence grading, confidence treatment, and reporting templates. The foundational v0.1 release prioritizes conceptual coherence, auditability, interpretive restraint, and governance relevance before empirical scaling or operational application.

dates.description: Initial public release of AI-SNF White Book v0.1. The PDF states January 2026; the metadata uses the last day of the month for a complete ISO date.

imprint.title: AI-Strategic Node Framework (AI-SNF): Conceptual and Methodological White Book

figures.title: Integrated Structural Architecture of AI-SNF and AI-SNI Outputs
figures.description: Illustrates the layered AI-SNF architecture: D1–D3 operational centrality, D4 governance-alignment and fragility, D5 forward-looking coupling potential, AI-SNI bounded composite output, exposure tier bands, confidence profile, and non-derivative structural class attribution.
figures.note: The figure emphasizes that AI-SNI outputs support contextual analysis but do not determine systemic role; structural class attribution cannot be inferred from composite scores, tier groups, or diagnostic extensions.

tables.title: Five Core Dimensions of AI-SNF
tables.description: Defines D1 Algorithmic Sensing and Early-Warning Centrality, D2 Predictive Model Leverage and Dependency, D3 Decision-Loop Temporal Advantage, D4 Infrastructure-Governance Asymmetry and Control, and D5 Resource-Data-Compute Coupling Potential.

tables.title: Composite Index Formula and Diagnostic Extensions
tables.description: Specifies reference AI-SNI as the authoritative bounded composite diagnostic output and distinguishes diagnostic extensions for governance fragility and weakest-link sensitivity from alternative index formulations.

tables.title: AI-SNI Exposure Tier Bands
tables.description: Defines four exposure-oriented tier bands for visualization and coarse screening: Tier 1 Critical Exposure (AI-SNI ≥ 0.80), Tier 2 High Exposure (0.65–0.80), Tier 3 Relevant Exposure (0.50–0.65), and Tier 4 Peripheral Exposure (AI-SNI < 0.50).
tables.note: Tier identifiers are non-ordinal and must not be treated as rankings, policy priorities, or measures of desirability.

tables.title: Structural Class Definitions (S / A / B / C)
tables.description: Defines structural classes as higher-order interpretive categories assessing systemic role, non-substitutability, cross-dimensional coupling, and failure-propagation implications independent of composite scores or tier placement.
tables.note: Structural class attribution is non-computable, non-ordinal, and cannot be mechanically inferred from AI-SNI values.

tables.title: Illustrative AI-SNI Calculation and Interpretation
tables.description: Provides a synthetic worked example showing dimension-level scoring, reference AI-SNI calculation, diagnostic extensions, tier assignment, structural tagging, and structural class attribution without making empirical claims about a real-world node.

tables.title: Core AI-Strategic Node Typologies and Typology Assignment Overview
tables.description: Summarizes Type I Concentrated Leverage with Weak Correction, Type II Concentrated Leverage with Institutional Buffering, Type III Diffuse Leverage with Participation Skew, and Type IV Peripheral or Latent Nodes.
tables.source: Author’s AI-SNF international benchmarking and node typology framework

tables.title: Uncertainty and Confidence Treatment
tables.description: Defines evidence grading, confidence propagation, uncertainty categories, interpretive constraints, and confidence override logic for AI-SNF and AI-SNI outputs.

tables.title: Typology Assignment Protocol
tables.description: Provides a structured protocol for assigning typology labels as diagnostic, non-prescriptive, non-ranking categories.

tables.title: AI-SNF / AI-SNI Reporting Template
tables.description: Provides a reporting template covering node identification, D1–D5 dimension profiles, evidence grades, confidence levels, AI-SNI composite output, diagnostic extensions, exposure tier, structural typology, structural class attribution, interpretation summary, and disclosure constraints.

tables.title: Abbreviation and Glossary
tables.description: Defines key AI-SNF and AI-SNI abbreviations and glossary terms used across the framework.
